info.name: MyFlatInfos – Polls & Surveys
info.description:
All API and Admin endpoints for the Polls & Surveys feature.

**Variables to set:**
- `api_url` – e.g. `http://your-domain.com/api`
- `admin_url` – e.g. `http://your-domain.com`
- `token` – Bearer token from user login (for User API)
- `csrf_token` – CSRF token from admin session (for Admin routes)
- `poll_id` – Poll ID to use in detail/action requests

item.name: 📱 User API
item.description: Mobile app endpoints. Requires: Authorization: Bearer {{token}} + flat must be selected via select-flat first.

request.description:
Returns all active, closed, and published polls for the user's building.

Returns:
- `id`, `title`, `description`, `type` (poll/survey)
- `structure` (single/multiple)
- `voting_type` (flat_based/user_based)
- `status` (active / expiring_soon / closed / published)
- `expiry_date`
- `has_voted` – whether this user/flat already voted
- `total_voters` – total unique votes cast so far
- `results_released` – true only when status = published
- `created_by_name`, `created_by_role`

response.name: 200 OK – Example
response.body:
{
  "polls": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "title": "Parking Bay Preference",
      "description": "Tell us your preferred parking arrangement.",
      "type": "poll",
      "structure": "single",
      "voting_type": "user_based",
      "status": "active",
      "expiry_date": "2026-04-15 18:00:00",
      "created_by_name": "John Smith",
      "created_by_role": "Building Admin",
      "has_voted": false,
      "total_voters": 12,
      "questions_count": 1,
      "results_released": false
    }
  ]
}

request.description:
Returns full poll with all questions and options.

**Required body:**
- `poll_id` – integer

Returns:
- All poll fields from get-polls
- `participation_pct` – % of eligible voters who have voted
- `questions[]` – array of questions with options
  - Each option has `is_selected: true` if this user already voted for it

**Note:** Results data (vote counts/%) is NOT included here. Use `get-poll-results` after results are released.

response.name: 200 OK – Example

request.description:
Submit the user's vote for a poll.

**Required body:**
- `poll_id` – integer
- `answers` – array of `{ question_id, option_id }`
  - For **single-structure** polls: 1 answer
  - For **multiple-structure** polls: one answer per question (all required)

**Rules:**
- Poll must be `active` and not expired
- User can only vote once (permanent, cannot change)
- `flat_based`: enforced per flat_id; `user_based`: enforced per user_id

**Returns:**
- `{ "message": "Vote cast successfully.", "has_voted": true }`

response.name: 422 – Already voted

request.description:
Fetch final results for a poll.

**Required body:**
- `poll_id` – integer

**Rules:**
- Returns 403 if status is NOT `published` (results not yet released by admin)
- Even users who didn't vote can view results once published

**Returns per question:**
- `total_votes`
- Options with `votes` count and `percentage`

response.name: 200 OK – Results

item.name: 🔧 Admin Panel (Building Admin)
item.description:
Web session routes for Building Admin (BA) / President.

**Auth:** Session cookie + CSRF token.
Add `_token: {{csrf_token}}` to every POST/DELETE body, or set `X-CSRF-TOKEN: {{csrf_token}}` header.

To get CSRF token: load any admin page and copy from `<meta name="csrf-token">` in the HTML head.

request.description:
Returns the Polls & Surveys index page.
Shows all polls for the admin's building with status, total voters, expiry, and action buttons.

urlencoded.value: Which parking arrangement do you prefer?

request.description:
Create a new poll or survey and save it as **Draft** (not visible to users yet).

**Required fields:**
- `title`
- `type`: `poll` or `survey`
- `structure`: `single` or `multiple`
- `voting_type`: `user_based` or `flat_based`
- `questions[0][question]`
- `questions[0][options][]` – minimum 2 options

**Optional:**
- `description`
- `expiry_date` – datetime-local format

**Multiple questions:** Add `questions[1][question]`, `questions[1][options][]`, etc. (only valid when `structure=multiple`)

**status_action:** `draft` = save only, `activate` = save and go live immediately

urlencoded.value: Vote for your preferred common area.
Which area do you use most?

request.description:
Create and immediately activate a poll. Users will be notified via push notification.

Same fields as *Save as Draft* but `status_action = activate`.

View poll details and live voting results (available to admin at **any status** — draft, active, closed, published).

Shows:
- Poll info (status, voting type, expiry, total voters)
- Per-option vote counts and % progress bars
- Action buttons (activate / close / release results / delete)

Activate a draft poll. Makes it visible and accepting votes from users.
Push notification is sent to all flat owners and tenants in the building.

**Rules:** Poll must be in `draft` status.

**Returns:** `{ "msg": "success", "status": "active" }`

Manually close an active poll. Voting stops immediately.
Users can no longer submit votes. Admin can then review results and release them.

**Note:** Polls also auto-close when `expiry_date` is reached (via scheduled command `polls:close-expired`).

**Returns:** `{ "msg": "success", "status": "closed" }`

Publish poll results so all users can see them.
Push notification is sent to all building users.

**Rules:**
- Poll must be `closed`
- **Only the creator** of the poll can release results (other admins cannot)

**Returns:** `{ "msg": "success", "status": "published" }`

response.name: 403 – Not the creator
422 – Not closed

request.description:
Update the expiry date of a poll.

**This is the ONLY field editable after creation.**

**Rules:**
- `expiry_date` must be a future date/time
- Cannot change expiry of `closed` or `published` polls

**Returns:** `{ "msg": "success", "expiry_date": "10 May 2026, 11:59 PM" }`

response.name: 422 – Closed/published poll

request.description:
Soft-delete a poll at **any stage**.
Deleted polls are hidden from users immediately (the app API filters by `deleted_at IS NULL`).

To **restore** a deleted poll: set `action = restore`.

**Returns:** `{ "msg": "success" }`

item.name: 🛡️ Super Admin Panel
item.description:
Same lifecycle endpoints as Building Admin but:
- **Building is selected via `building_id` field** in the create form (SA manages all buildings)
- `created_by_role` is automatically set to `Super Admin`
- No middleware restriction — SA is authenticated via the `admin` session middleware

All action URLs (`/poll/{id}/activate` etc.) are identical to the BA panel.

request.description: Lists polls across all buildings. Use `?building_id=X` query param to filter by a specific building.

urlencoded.value: Help us improve the building rules.
Are you satisfied with the current noise rules?

request.description:
Create a poll from the Super Admin panel.

**Key difference from BA:** `building_id` is required in the body (SA selects the target building).
`created_by_role` is automatically stored as `'Super Admin'`.

item.name: 📊 Status Flow Reference
item.description:
Poll status lifecycle:

`draft` → `active` → `closed` → `published`

| Status | Visible to Users | Voting Open | Results Visible |
|---|---|---|---|
| draft | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| active | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| expiring_soon* | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| closed | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| published | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |

*`expiring_soon` is a computed display status when `active` and within 24 hrs of `expiry_date`. Stored as `active` in DB.

**Auto-close:** Scheduler runs `polls:close-expired` every minute. Any `active` poll past its `expiry_date` is auto-moved to `closed`.
